title: Charlotte · The Flat Line
subtitle: FBI UCR 1985–2016 + CMPD NIBRS 2017–2026 · 14 police divisions · real locations

hook.line: Charlotte crime, flat for eight years
hook.sub: CMPD incidents · the citywide line is flat — the map underneath is not

punchline.sub: +0.2% in eight years · reported criminal incidents (CMPD), the same measure

copy.seamExplain: The ruler changes here — not the city. Through 2016 the gray area is the FBI's classic 'index' crimes (murder, robbery, assault, burglary, theft). From 2017 the figures are CMPD's own NIBRS incident reports — a broader net, so the level steps up. Compare the shape within one color, never across the dashed line.
copy.chapter2Kicker: CHAPTER 2 · THE LAST 5 YEARS · CMPD NIBRS
copy.chapter2Caption: Monthly reports, 2021–2026. Every dot is a real reported incident location, block-level, sampled.
copy.transitionDesc: The chart counted per year. From here the map counts per month across 14 police divisions — every dot a real reported location, anonymized to the block.
copy.methodRecentSub: the last five years · month by month · real locations
copy.methodDotsSub: Every dot is a real reported incident location, block-anonymized by CMPD. 99.9% map to one of 14 police divisions — the rest counted, disclosed, never invented.
copy.methodFootnote: FBI UCR (through 2016) and CMPD NIBRS incidents (2017+) are different measures — shown as two chapters, not one line. Non-criminal 800-series and unfounded reports are excluded and disclosed.
copy.quizQuestion: Which Charlotte police division reports the fewest Group A crimes?
copy.sourceLine: Data: Charlotte-Mecklenburg Police Department via the City of Charlotte Open Data Portal · block-anonymized locations
copy.coverageText: 99.9% of records mapped to a police division · 800-series & unfounded reports excluded
copy.creditsSources: Sources: Charlotte-Mecklenburg Police Department incident data (2017–present) via the City of Charlotte Open Data Portal & FBI UCR (1985–2016) via the FBI Crime Data Explorer (ORI NC0600100). Police divisions: CMPD official boundaries. Excluded and disclosed: 128,848 non-criminal 800-series local reports and 21,392 reports cleared as unfounded. Locations are block-anonymized by the source. FBI UCR index crimes and CMPD NIBRS incidents are different measures, shown as two chapters and never equated. Basemap: © OpenStreetMap contributors (ODbL).

historyNotes.text: 2007: the FBI-era peak — 58,900 index crimes reported.
2014: 35,784 — the lowest count since 1986; then the line turns back up.
2016: 43,512 — the last FBI year, already climbing; CMPD's own record begins next year.
2025: 75,179 — within 0.2% of where CMPD's count started in 2017.

annotations.text: Every dot is a real reported incident location — anonymized to the block by CMPD open data.
July 2023 — the busiest Group A month of the last five years: 6,097.
University City — Charlotte's busiest division for reported Group A crime, and its fastest riser: +17% since 2022.
Airport: down 33% since 2022 — the city's biggest division drop.
Reported Group A is up slightly citywide: 62,613 (2022) → 64,625 (2025), +3%.
